question: Ethereum Up or Down - January 23, 1:15AM-1:30AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Ethereum price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the ETH/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/eth-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream ETH/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Dota 2: Zero Tenacity vs Valinor (BO3) - PGL Wallachia: Western Europe Open Qualifier 1 Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Zero Tenacity and Valinor in the PGL Wallachia: Western Europe Open Qualifier 1 Playoffs, scheduled for January 22 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Zero Tenacity" if Zero Tenacity win the match against Valinor.

This market will resolve to "Valinor" if Valinor win the match against Zero Tenacity.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.liquipedia.net/dota2/Main_Page.

question: Set Handicap: Shelton (-1.5) vs Vacherot (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Ben Shelton and Valentin Vacherot in the Australian Open ATP, scheduled for January 23 2026.
This market will resolve to "Shelton" if Ben Shelton wins by 2 or more sets than Valentin Vacherot, based on the final completed score. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Vacherot."
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Grand Slam results.
groupItemTitle: Australian Open Men's: Ben Shelton vs Valentin Vacherot Set Handicap +/-1.5

events.title: Australian Open Men's: Ben Shelton vs Valentin Vacherot
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Ben Shelton and Valentin Vacherot in the Australian Open ATP, scheduled for January 22 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Shelton' if Ben Shelton advances against Valentin Vacherot.

This market will resolve to 'Vacherot' if Valentin Vacherot advances against Ben Shelton.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Map Handicap: FORZE.R (-1.5) vs Acend (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between FORZE Reload and Acend in the European Pro League Series 4 Group B, scheduled for January 22 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "FORZE Reload" if FORZE Reload wins 2 or more maps than Acend in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Acend".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

question: Will the highest temperature in London be 5°C or below on January 24?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 24 Jan '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 24 Jan '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

question: Ethereum Up or Down - January 24, 6AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the ETH/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Game Handicap: DYG (-1.5) vs Royal Never Give Up (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the Honor of Kings match between Douyu Gaming and Royal Never Give Up in the King Pro League Stage 1 Group 2, scheduled for January 22 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Douyu Gaming" if Douyu Gaming wins 2 or more games than Royal Never Give Up in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Royal Never Give Up".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.
